name: Crispy Treats
directions: Spray 2 9-x-13 inch baking pans.  Put one-half of cereal and
one-half of nuts in each pan.
Melt butter; add sugar and Karo.  Spread mixture over cereal and nuts, dividing equally between the pans.
Bake 1 hour at 250 degrees, turning every 15 minutes.
Turn out on foil that has been sprayed with non-stick spray.  Break apart.  When completely cooled, store in a lightly covered container.

similar.name: Baked Scallops in Honey and Butter

name: Salad Fantastico
directions: Toss all ingredients together and serve.

name: Luau Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients, except bread and process in a food processor.
Scoop out the loaf of bread to form a bread bowl.
Place dip into bread bowl.
Arrange pieces of bread center arrange around bread bowl on a tray or plate.

name: Creamy Cinnamon Fruit Dip
directions: Add all ingredients in a medium bowl and beat until smooth.
Spoon into serving dish, cover, and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serve with various fruit.

name: Baked Scallops in Honey and Butter
directions: Cut scallops in half or into quarters, depending on their size.
Dry well with paper towels.
Melt the butter with the honey and set aside.
Roll the scallops in the bread or cracker crumbs and divide them equally into 4-6 individual greased ramekins or small baking dishes.
Whisk the oil into the honey-butter blend and drizzle evenly over each portion of scallops.
Bake for 10-15 minutes at 475 degrees F.

name: Snapper Tropical
directions: **Preheat your oven to 350 degrees.

Heat the vegetable oil in a large oven proof saute pan while you lightly dredge the snapper in the flour and shake off all excess. Sautee both sides of the snapper filets in the oil till lightly browned then discard the vegetable oil. Deglaze the pan with the fish stock and the rum and let reduce for 2-3 minutes, add the basil, salt, pepper, orange juice and butter. Add the fresh fruit, and place in the oven for a final 4-5 minutes of cooking.
Plate the fish, top with the fresh fruit sauce.

name: Patrick's Alternative Duck Rub
directions: I grind up my raisins pretty fine in the food processor by pulsing the blender. If your raisins are so moist that they "ball up" in the food processor, try mincing them finely by hand with a French knife.
Blend all rub ingredients.
Rub the ducks inside and out with the blend and allow them to sit for a few minutes prior to roasting. Toss any extra rub inside the body cavity of the ducks.
TIP: As juices form in the roaster during cooking, add in a stick of butter and baste the bird 2 or 3 times before it gets done.

name: Baked Sesame Ginger Salmon
directions: Preheat oven to 400 degrees and lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Combine: marinade, sesame oil, and peanut butter. Mix well (you might need to 'nuke it for a few seconds) until creamy.
Cut each salmon fillet in half to give 4 nice salmon chunks. Spoon the mixture over the fish, evenly dividing among the 4 chunks.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the fish flakes with a fork.

similar.name: Baked Scallops in Honey and Butter

name: Angel Food Cake Wraps
directions: Lay out 6 wrappers at a time.
Keep others covered in a towel.
Layer the following in the wrapper.
1 angel food cube.
1/2 tsp brown sugar.
4 or 5 marshmellows.
Pinch of cinnamon on top.
Wet your fingers in cold water and brush the edges of the wonton.
Bring two edges opposite sides together,in a triangle and seal the two sides.
Prepare 6 at a time, cover under a damp towel.
Preheat oil in fryer to 375*.
Fry 6 to 8 at a time.
Place on a paper towelled platter. Keep warm in the oven at 300*.
Serve warm, not hot.

name: Chunky Chocolate Drops
directions: Preheat the oven to 160c gas 3. You need 2 baking trays, measure and cut to size 2 greaseproof paper sheets, then greas them.
Melt the chocolate and butter over a low heat (DO NOT USE the microwave for this), remove from the heat and allow to cool slightly.
Mix the egg and sugar together until creamy. Gradually add the chocolate mixture. Add the dry ingredients and vanilla essence. Finally add the nuts and choc chips.

Drop heaped tablespoons of the mixture on the sheets approx 4-6 on each tray and flatten spaced well apart. Bake for 8-10mins. Remove from the oven and allow to cool slightly on the tray before transfering to a wire rack. If they're not cooled on the tray first they will break.

name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Bars
directions: Line 9x13 pan with wax paper (I use a glass pan).
Melt 1 1/2 sticks of butter in microwave.
Add graham cracker crumbs, peanut butter, and sugar to melted butter.
Spread evenly in pan.
Melt 1 1/2 sticks butter and chocolate chips on stove on medium-low.
Pour evenly over peanut butter mixture.
Cool in refrigerator at least 6 hours.
Cut into small squares. (They are easier to cut if they don't get too cold.).

name: No Sugar Added -- Sweetened Condensed Milk
directions: Whisk together ingredients and simmer and stir over low heat until it thickens.
Allow to cool and use as you would condensed milk.

name: Friendship Muffins
directions: Preheat oven to 350'.
Mix batter, 2 cups flour, oil, baking soda, baking powder, sugar, eggs, cinnamon and vanilla and stir well.
Add nuts, apples, raisins, and/or blueberries and mix well.
In a separate bowl, mix brown sugar, 1/2 cup flour and margarine well.
Put batter into muffin tins well greased if not using liners. Sprinkle with topping on each muffin and bake for 15-20 minutes or until done.

name: 5 Minute Chocolate Mug Cake
directions: Add dry ingredients to a large coffee mug, and mix well. Add the egg and mix thoroughly. Pour in the milk and oil and mix well. Add the chocolate chips (if using) and vanilla extract, and mix again.

Put your mug in the microwave and cook for 3 minutes at 1000 watts (high). The cake will rise over the top of the mug, but don't be alarmed! Allow to cool a little, and tip out onto a plate if desired.

name: Sweet Quark Pasta Casserole
directions: Cook pasta in slightly salted water until al dente (firm to the bite). Drain well.
Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F.
In a bowl mix quark, egg, icing sugar, butter and vanilla pudding powder. Gently fold in pasta.
Spoon into individual ramekins or a casserole dish.
Bake on the lowest rack for 30-35 minutes until custard has set and top is slightly browned. Serve with sauce of your choice (mango, chocolate, caramel, raspberry) or eat as is.

similar.name: No Sugar Added -- Sweetened Condensed Milk

name: Cherry Crush Slushie
directions: Put 1 cup of Orange Crush Cherry Soda in a blender.
Add 2 teaspoons of sugar.
Add 12 ice cubes.
Put the blender on Crush Ice until chunky and nice.
Put blender on whatever setting you like until it looks right to you.

similar.name: No Sugar Added -- Sweetened Condensed Milk

name: Peanut Butter Fruit Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stirring well.  Cover and chill thoroughly.  Serve dip with an assortment of fresh fruit. (Apple and Banana seem to go over best with children!).

name: Blackberry Jam No Pectin Required
directions: Mix fruit and sugar.
Let stand 10 minutes or overnight ( either works)
Cook ten minutes and turn off stove.
Stir in gelatin
Put in hot jars.

name: Yummiest Yogurt
directions: 1. In a cereal bowl, mix yogurt and honey.  Add raisins/Craisins and pretzels.

similar.name: No Sugar Added -- Sweetened Condensed Milk

name: Valentine's Day Puppy Chow!
directions: Put 1/2 cup powdered sugar and 1/2 cup cake mix 1 gallon size zip-lock bag, do to another bag.
Put 2 1/2 cups rice chex cereal into a medium sized bowl.
Melt white chocolate and stir into cereal (be careful not to break cereal).
Pour white chocolate cereal into 1 of the powder sugar bags, and shake until all are coated.
Do the same process to the red melts and rest of cereal.
Mix both cereals, and M&Ms in a large bowl.
EAT!

name: The Classic S-More
directions: Put all 4 marshmallows on (clean) wooden sticks.
Roast for about 5 minutes (if the marshmallow sets fire or gets burnt take it out of the fire).
Get 4 of the crackers and put the cooked marshmallows on them.
Put 1 piece of milk chocolate on each marshmallow.
Put the remaining crackers on the top.
